subject: Gaming
title: World of Warcraft: A Digital Nation
description: A micro-lesson that makes World of Warcraft: A Digital Nation usable.

hook.text: Twelve million people paid monthly to live in a fantasy world. WoW became gaming's largest community.

buildup.text: Launched in 2004, WoW turned the MMO from a niche genre into a global cultural phenomenon.

discovery.text: Players formed guilds, economies, and hierarchies. Some met spouses. Others lost jobs to the addiction.

twist.text: A corrupted blood plague spread in 2005. Epidemiologists studied it as a real pandemic model.

climax.text: WoW earned over $9 billion in subscriptions and proved online worlds could sustain millions.

punchline.text: Twelve million paid to live in a fantasy world. A digital nation.

quiz.question: What unexpected research came from World of Warcraft?
options: Epidemiologists studied an in-game plague as a pandemic model
